name: Earn2Trade
description_short: Education-focused futures prop firm with structured programs, virtual trading boot camps, and funded accounts through Helios Trading Partners.
description_long:
## What Is Earn2Trade?

Earn2Trade is a futures-focused proprietary trading firm that funds traders who pass its evaluation programs. Founded in 2016 and registered in Sheridan, Wyoming, the company operates as Earn2Trade LLC and has built a reputation as one of the more education-oriented prop firms in the space. Unlike competitors that simply hand you a simulated account and say "good luck," Earn2Trade positions itself as a training ground for aspiring futures traders.

The company partners with Helios Trading Partners to provide funded accounts to successful candidates. As of 2025, Earn2Trade reports funding over 6,400 traders and holds a 4.7 TrustPilot score across 4,600+ reviews — numbers that place it among the more established players in the prop firm industry.

## Core Programs and How They Work

Earn2Trade offers two main evaluation paths, plus an educational course for complete beginners.

**The Gauntlet Mini** is the streamlined option. You trade a virtual futures account for a minimum of 10 trading days, hit the profit target without violating drawdown rules, and earn funding. It is designed for traders who already have a strategy and just need to prove consistency. Account sizes range from $25,000 to $50,000.

**The Trader Career Path (TCP)** is the flagship program and where Earn2Trade differentiates itself most. This is a progressive evaluation that starts at $25,000 and allows you to scale up through multiple levels, eventually reaching a $400,000 funded account with a fixed drawdown. The TCP includes educational resources, mentorship elements, and a more structured advancement system. It is the better choice for traders who want a defined growth trajectory rather than a one-shot evaluation.

Both programs evaluate you on real market conditions using futures contracts. You will trade through platforms like NinjaTrader, R|Trader, or other supported options — you cannot use just any platform.

## Pricing Breakdown

Earn2Trade charges monthly subscription fees during the evaluation period:

- **Gauntlet Mini $25K account:** $99 per month
- **Gauntlet Mini $50K account:** $149 per month
- **Trader Career Path $25K starting level:** $199 per month
- **Beginner Crash Course:** Available separately for newer traders

There are no annual discount options listed. These fees recur monthly until you either pass the evaluation or cancel. This is standard for prop firms, but the costs add up quickly if you need multiple months or attempts. A trader who takes three months to pass the $50K Gauntlet Mini has spent $447 before ever seeing a funded account.

Once funded, the profit split is **80/20** — you keep 80% of profits. This is decent but not industry-leading. Apex Trader Funding offers 100% of the first $25,000 in profits and 90/10 thereafter. Topstep offers 90/10 from the start on some plans. The 80/20 split at Earn2Trade is the weakest point of its compensation structure.

## The Numbers You Need to See

Earn2Trade publishes its pass rates in the fine print, and they deserve attention. In 2025, **only 8.89% of candidates passed** either the Trader Career Path or Gauntlet Mini evaluations. That means roughly 91 out of every 100 people who paid for an evaluation did not pass.

Of those who did pass, **only 5.23% traded on a live account** — the remaining 94.77% were placed on LiveSim accounts, which simulate live conditions but do not involve real capital. And among all funded accounts (live and LiveSim combined), only about **18% had at least one withdrawal**.

These statistics are not unique to Earn2Trade — the entire prop firm industry has similar dynamics. But they are a reality check. The majority of evaluation fees collected by prop firms come from traders who never reach a funded account. Factor this into your decision.

## What Earn2Trade Does Well

The **education-first approach** is genuinely useful. Most prop firms treat education as an afterthought or upsell. Earn2Trade bakes it into the Trader Career Path, which means newer traders get structured learning alongside their evaluation. This is not a replacement for independent study, but it adds real value.

The **progressive scaling model** in the TCP is well-designed. Starting at $25K and working up to $400K with a fixed drawdown gives traders a clear growth roadmap. You are not stuck at one account size forever.

**Customer support** is consistently praised in reviews. Multiple Trustpilot testimonials highlight fast response times and willingness to work with traders on billing and timing issues.

The **10-day minimum evaluation** on the Gauntlet Mini is one of the shortest in the industry. If you are a consistent trader, you can move from evaluation to funding quickly.

Earn2Trade also supports **crypto payouts** for withdrawals, which adds flexibility for traders who prefer that option.

## Where Earn2Trade Falls Short

**Futures only.** If you trade stocks, options, forex, or crypto spot markets, Earn2Trade has nothing for you. This is a significant limitation compared to firms like FTMO, which evaluates forex and other markets.

The **80/20 profit split** is below what several competitors offer. When you are trading with someone else's capital and have already paid evaluation fees, keeping only 80% of profits stings — especially when alternatives offer 90% or more.

**Platform restrictions** mean you must use their approved trading platforms. If you have a preferred platform that is not on the list, you are out of luck.

**Monthly fees with no annual discount** make extended evaluations expensive. There is no way to reduce cost by committing to a longer period upfront.

The **LiveSim reality** is worth emphasizing. Even after passing, the vast majority of funded traders are placed on simulated accounts rather than live ones. While the profit mechanics may be similar, this distinction matters to traders who want to know their capital is real.

## Who Should Use Earn2Trade

Earn2Trade is a strong fit for **aspiring futures traders who value structure and education**. If you are relatively new to futures, the Trader Career Path provides a guided framework that most prop firms lack. It is also good for **disciplined traders who already have a futures strategy** and want the shortest possible path to funding via the Gauntlet Mini.

Traders who prefer **clear progression milestones** will appreciate the TCP's scaling system. And anyone frustrated by prop firms with poor customer service will find Earn2Trade's support refreshing.

## Who Should NOT Use Earn2Trade

If you trade **anything other than futures**, look elsewhere. Earn2Trade will not serve you.

If you are **primarily motivated by profit split**, firms like Apex Trader Funding or Topstep offer better terms. The 80/20 split is a real disadvantage for high-performing traders.

If you are a **complete beginner with no trading experience**, the evaluation programs will likely take your money without results. The 8.89% pass rate includes experienced traders. Start with their Beginner Crash Course or independent education before paying for an evaluation.

## Earn2Trade vs. Topstep vs. Apex Trader Funding

**Topstep** is the closest direct competitor. Both focus on futures, both have structured evaluations, and both have strong reputations. Topstep offers a 90/10 profit split on some plans, which beats Earn2Trade's 80/20. However, Earn2Trade's Trader Career Path with its progressive scaling to $400K is a feature Topstep does not match directly.

**Apex Trader Funding** is the budget-friendly disruptor. It frequently runs promotions that bring evaluation costs well below Earn2Trade's pricing, and its profit split (100% of first $25K, then 90/10) is the most generous in the space. However, Apex provides virtually no educational support and has faced more scrutiny around its evaluation and payout practices.

Earn2Trade sits in the middle: more structured and educational than Apex, slightly more expensive with a less favorable split than either competitor, but with the best defined career progression path of the three.

## The Bottom Line

Earn2Trade is a legitimate, well-established futures prop firm that stands out for its educational approach and structured career path. The 4.7 TrustPilot rating and 9+ years in business are not accidents — the company delivers on its promises within the boundaries of what prop firms actually are.

But be clear-eyed about what you are buying. Roughly 91% of traders who start an evaluation will not pass. The profit split is below the competition. And "funded" often means LiveSim rather than live capital.

If you are a futures trader who wants structure, education, and a clear path from evaluation to a $400K account, Earn2Trade is worth the investment. If you just want the cheapest path to funding with the best profit split, Apex or Topstep may serve you better. The right choice depends on whether you value the journey or just the destination.
